intro.en: Where three rivers converge beneath the world's largest stone Buddha. Leshan (ancient Jiazhou) blends misty mountains, thousand-year temples, and a food scene that rivals Chengdu itself. Slow down — this city lives at its own pace.
intro.zh: 三江汇流处，端坐着世界最大石刻佛像。乐山（古称嘉州）将山水仙境、千年古刹和不输成都的美食融为一体。慢下来——这座城有自己的节奏。

desc.en: At 71 meters tall, the Leshan Giant Buddha is the largest stone-carved Maitreya Buddha in the world — carved into a cliff face where three rivers meet. For over 1,300 years, it has sat in serene silence, watching the waters of the Min, Qingyi, and Dadu rivers converge below. The scale is awe-inspiring: each foot is large enough for 100 people to sit on. Climb the Nine-Turn Plank Path alongside the Buddha for the best view.
desc.zh: 71米高的乐山大佛是世界上最大的石刻弥勒坐像，雕凿在三江交汇处的悬崖峭壁上。1300多年来，它静坐于此，俯瞰岷江、青衣江、大渡河奔涌交汇。规模令人震撼——仅一只脚上就能站满百人。沿大佛右侧的九曲栈道下行，是仰视大佛的最佳角度。

highlight.en: Nine-Turn Plank Path — walk right next to the Buddha's head and feet
highlight.zh: 九曲栈道——紧贴大佛从头走到脚

tips.en: Go early (before 8 AM) to beat the crowds and see the morning mist over the river. Boat cruise (¥70) gives the best full-body view.
tips.zh: 早上8点前到避开人流，看江面晨雾。坐游船（¥70）能看到大佛全身最佳角度。

location.en: Downtown Leshan, at the confluence of three rivers

tags.zh: 世界遗产 · 地标 · 清晨

desc.en: One of China's Four Sacred Buddhist Mountains, Emeishan rises 3,099 meters above sea level — a realm of misty peaks, ancient temples hidden in bamboo forests, and wild monkeys that demand your snacks. The Golden Summit (Jinding) at dawn offers one of the most transcendent experiences in all of China — a sea of clouds stretching to the horizon, the sun rising in a blaze of gold, and if you're lucky, the legendary Buddha's Halo.
desc.zh: 中国四大佛教名山之一，海拔3099米——云雾缭绕的山峰、竹林深处藏古寺、向你讨零食的野生猴群。金顶日出是中国最超凡的体验之一——云海一望无际，朝阳金光万丈，运气好还能看到传说中的佛光。

highlight.en: Golden Summit sunrise — sea of clouds and Buddha's Halo
highlight.zh: 金顶日出——云海+佛光奇观

hours.en: Open all day (cable car 5:00 AM – 6:00 PM)

ticket.en: ¥160 (high season), ¥110 (low season); cable car ¥65 up / ¥55 down

tips.en: Take the cable car up and hike down — the trail passes 20+ temples. Wear good shoes; there are thousands of steps. Bring snacks for the monkeys (and hold your bags tight).
tips.zh: 索道上山+徒步下山——沿途经过20多座寺庙。穿好鞋，台阶无数。给猴子带零食（但包要抓紧）。

location.zh: 峨眉山市，距乐山市区约40公里

tags.zh: 世界遗产 · 自然 · 日出

name.en: Jiazhou Ancient City & Night River View

desc.en: Leshan's old town, Jiazhou, hugs the riverfront with grey-tiled roofs and weathered wooden facades. By day, wander the narrow lanes where locals dry peppers on bamboo trays and play mahjong under ancient banyan trees. By night, the riverside promenade lights up — the Buddha is illuminated across the water, bridges shimmer in gold, and night cruise boats glide through the reflections.
desc.zh: 乐山老城区嘉州沿江而建，青瓦民居、斑驳木门。白天穿梭小巷，看居民在竹匾上晒辣椒，在老榕树下搓麻将。入夜后江畔步道华灯初上——大佛被灯光照亮对岸，大桥金光闪烁，夜游船只划过倒影。

highlight.en: Night cruise under the illuminated Giant Buddha
highlight.zh: 夜游船——看灯光勾勒的大佛轮廓

tips.en: The night view is best right after sunset — about 30 minutes of twilight blue. Take the 8 PM cruise slot.
tips.zh: 黄昏后30分钟蓝调时刻最美。建议坐8点的船。

tags.zh: 夜景 · 摄影 · 浪漫

desc.en: Leshan's signature dish — lacquered duck with a glistening sugar glaze. The skin is crispy and sweet, the meat tender and savory. Each bite is a perfect balance of sweet and salty. The aroma alone can draw you across the street. It's a must-try that rivals Beijing's roast duck in its own way.
desc.zh: 乐山的招牌硬菜——油亮红润的糖衣脆皮，紧实细嫩的鸭肉。每一口都是甜咸交织的完美平衡。香气能飘满整条街，绝不输北京烤鸭。

where.en: Found at dedicated roast duck shops across the city — follow the sweet aroma
where.zh: 遍布全城的烤鸭专门店——循着甜香走

must_try.en: Ask for the drumstick — the best part, with the crispiest skin. Eat it while it's hot, fresh out of the oil.
must_try.zh: 要个鸭腿——最精华部位，皮最脆。趁热吃，刚出锅的最佳。

desc.en: Leshan's most iconic street snack — skewers of meat and vegetables soaked in a bubbling vat of chili oil or Sichuan peppercorn broth. It's served cold, making it the perfect summer appetizer. The Sichuan peppercorn version numbs your lips with every bite; the red oil version hits you with pure chili fragrance. One skewer leads to fifty. You've been warned.
desc.zh: 乐山最经典街头小吃——竹签串着各种荤素，浸泡在红油或藤椒汤底里。冷吃，夏天绝配。藤椒味麻到嘴唇跳舞，红油味香到停不下来。一串接一串，五十串打底。

where.en: Every corner snack street in Leshan — look for the big pots of skewers
where.zh: 乐山的每一条小吃街——看到大盆竹签就是了

must_try.en: Go for the duck gizzards and beef tripe — they absorb the most flavor. Don't be shy; grab a handful.
must_try.zh: 鸭胗和毛肚最入味。别客气，一把一把拿。

desc.en: A steaming bowl of beef, offal, and tripe in a clear, fragrant broth simmered for hours with over 20 herbs and spices. The name literally means 'cross-legged beef' — because old patrons used to sit cross-legged on low stools while eating. The broth is the star: light yet profoundly savory, a testament to the Sichuan art of slow cooking.
desc.zh: 一大碗热腾腾的牛肉、牛杂、毛肚，在20多种草药香料熬制的清汤中翻滚。名字来源于老食客坐在矮凳上跷着脚吃。汤是灵魂——清淡却鲜美无比，彰显川式慢炖的功力。

where.en: Leshan's Qiaojiao Niurou restaurants — look for steaming pots at the entrance
where.zh: 乐山的跷脚牛肉店——门口有大锅冒蒸汽的就是

must_try.en: Order the mixed platter (niú zá) for a taste of everything. Dip in dry chili powder for the full experience.
must_try.zh: 点牛杂拼盘——一锅吃遍所有部位。蘸干辣椒面才是正确吃法。

desc.en: For over two millennia, Leshan has been a crossroads of culture and commerce along the ancient Southern Silk Road. The city's old streets — like Shizhong Laojie — still echo with the footsteps of merchants who once carried Sichuan tea and silk southward. Buddhist culture runs deep: the Giant Buddha is just the most visible of countless temples and grottoes scattered across the region. Poetry, calligraphy, and local opera thrive in the lanes.
desc.zh: 两千多年来，乐山一直是南方丝绸之路上的文化重镇。老街巷陌间——如市中老街——仿佛还能听到当年茶马商队的脚步声。佛教文化深厚：大佛只是这片区域无数寺庙和石窟中最显眼的一座。诗词、书法、川剧在街巷间生生不息。

significance.en: 2000+ years of history as a key hub on the Southern Silk Road
significance.zh: 2000多年历史，南方丝绸之路重要节点

best_time.zh: 四季皆宜——文化不分季节

experience.en: Walk Shizhong old street, visit a local teahouse, and catch a Sichuan opera performance
experience.zh: 逛市中老街、泡茶馆、看一场川剧

climate.en: Spring (Mar–May) & Autumn (Sep–Nov) — mild, clear, perfect for hiking and outdoor sightseeing. Summer is hot and humid; winter is cold and damp.
climate.zh: 春秋最佳（3-5月、9-11月）——温和晴朗，适合登山和户外游览。夏季湿热，冬季阴冷潮湿。

transit.en: Buses and taxis within the city. Leshan is compact — the Giant Buddha and old town are walkable. To reach Mount Emei, take bus route 601 (1 hour) or hire a taxi (¥80–100).
transit.zh: 市内公交和出租车。乐山不大——大佛和老城区步行可达。去峨眉山坐601路（1小时）或打车（¥80–100）。

intercity.en: Chengdu to Leshan: high-speed rail (50 min, ¥54) or bus (2h). The train station is "Leshan" (not Leshan North).
intercity.zh: 成都到乐山：高铁50分钟（¥54）或大巴2小时。买票到'乐山站'（不是乐山北）。

tips.en: Eat ALL the snacks — Leshan is one of China's great street food cities. The Giant Buddha boat tour is worth every yuan. Mount Emei gets cold at the summit even in summer — bring a jacket. Watch out for monkey bag-snatchers on Emeishan.
tips.zh: 吃遍所有小吃——乐山是中国最顶级的街头美食城市之一。大佛游船票绝对值。峨眉山金顶夏天也冷——带外套。小心峨眉山的猴子抢包。

practicalGuide.en:
Getting there from Chengdu: High-speed rail from Chengdu East to Leshan station — 50 minutes, ¥54. Trains run every 20-30 minutes. The station is called "Leshan" (乐山站), not Leshan North. If you miss the train, buses from Chengdu Xinnanmen station take 2 hours (¥45).

Giant Buddha logistics: Two ways to see it: (1) Walk the Nine-Turn Plank Path (九曲栈道) — ¥80, takes 1-2 hours, up-close view of the Buddha from head to toe. (2) Boat cruise — ¥70, 30 minutes, best full-body view from the river. Do both if you have time. The plank path gets crowded by 9 AM — arrive at 7:30 when the gates open.

Day trip vs overnight: Leshan works as a day trip from Chengdu, but stay overnight if you want to eat properly and catch the night river view. Local hotels near the old town cost ¥150-300/night — simple but clean. The night food scene alone is worth the stay.

Mount Emei logistics: Bus 601 from Leshan to Emeishan scenic area (1 hour, ¥12). Cable car to Golden Summit: ¥65 up, ¥55 down. Summit temperature is 10-15°C cooler than the base — bring a jacket even in summer. The monkeys are aggressive — do NOT carry open food or plastic bags they can see.
practicalGuide.zh:
成都到乐山：成都东站坐高铁到乐山站——50分钟，¥54。每20-30分钟一班。站名叫"乐山站"（不是乐山北站）。没赶上的话，成都新南门车站坐大巴2小时（¥45）。

大佛怎么玩：两条路线：(1)走九曲栈道——¥80，1-2小时，从大佛头顶走到脚边，近距离感受体量。(2)坐游船——¥70，30分钟，江上看大佛全身最佳角度。有时间两个都做。栈道9点后人挤人——7:30开门就到。

一日游还是住一晚？乐山可以从成都当天来回，但想吃好喝好再看个夜景观音就住一晚。老城区附近酒店¥150-300/晚——简单但干净。乐山的夜宵值得你住一晚。

峨眉山怎么去：乐山坐601路到峨眉山景区（1小时，¥12）。索道上金顶：上¥65/下¥55。金顶温度比山下低10-15°C——夏天也带外套。峨眉山的猴子很凶——别让它们看到你手里有吃的或塑料袋。

honestTips.en:
The plank path is NOT for everyone. The Nine-Turn Plank Path alongside the Giant Buddha is narrow, steep, and in peak season you could be stuck in a slow-moving line for 1+ hour. If you have mobility issues, claustrophobia, or severe heat sensitivity — skip the path and just take the boat cruise. The boat actually gives you a better photo anyway.

Sweet Glazed Duck is best eaten fresh. It loses its crispiness within 2 hours of cooking. If you buy it as takeaway and eat it later at your hotel, the skin will be soft and chewy. Eat it at the shop, hot from the fryer, or skip it entirely.

Emeishan summit is crowded at sunrise. Everyone wants that Golden Summit sunrise photo. The viewing platforms fill up by 5:30 AM. If you're not an early bird, skip the sunrise and come up at 10 AM when the crowds thin out — the sea of clouds is still there, and you'll have room to breathe.

Mosquitoes at the Giant Buddha. The Buddha sits at a river confluence — lots of standing water = lots of mosquitoes. Bring repellent. You will be standing still on the plank path, and they will find you.
honestTips.zh:
九曲栈道不适合所有人。大佛旁的栈道窄、陡，旺季能堵在上面排一个多小时。如果你腿脚不方便、有幽闭恐惧症、或者怕热——别走栈道，坐游船就够了。说实话游船拍照角度更好。

甜皮鸭必须趁热吃。出锅2小时后皮就软了不脆了。如果你买打包带回酒店吃，皮会又软又韧，你会怀疑它为什么这么有名。在店里趁热吃，刚从油锅捞出来就吃，否则别买。

峨眉山金顶日出全是人。每个人都想看金顶日出。观景台早上5:30就站满了。起不来的话，10点再上山——云海还在，空气还好。

大佛脚底蚊子多。大佛在三江汇流处——有水就有蚊子。带驱蚊液。你在栈道上站着不动，它们会来找你的。

localSecrets.en:
Shizhong old street morning market. Behind the main tourist street, the old town comes alive at 6 AM with a farmers market that hasn't changed in decades. Fresh vegetables, live poultry, handmade tofu, and the best Shanxi rice noodles in town. The street food stalls here cost half of what you'll pay near the Buddha. Go hungry.

Hejiang Tower (合江楼) at sunset. The best free view of Leshan. Climb this riverside tower for a panoramic view of the three rivers converging, with the Giant Buddha on your left and the old town on your right. Best time: 30 minutes before sunset. Bring a beer from the convenience store downstairs.

Emeishan back-road hiking trail. Instead of the cable car, take the trail starting from Wannian Temple (万年寺) — a 4-hour hike through bamboo forests, past hidden waterfalls, and ending at the Golden Summit. You'll pass 10+ ancient temples that 99% of tourists never see. Start at 7 AM. Bring water and snacks — there are few shops on this route.

Late-night Bobo chicken in the alleys. The best Bobo Chicken in Leshan isn't at a restaurant — it's the grandmother who sets up a single pot on a street corner near the old town at dusk and sells out in 2 hours. Look for the cart with a queue of locals, not tourists. ¥2 per skewer, cash only.
localSecrets.zh:
市中老街早市。主街背后，早上6点就有几十年来没变过的菜市场。新鲜蔬菜、活禽、手工豆腐、全城最好吃的酸辣粉。这里的早摊价格只有大佛景区的一半。空腹去。

合江楼看日落。乐山最好的免费观景台。爬上这座江边楼阁，三江汇流尽收眼底——左边是大佛，右边是老城。最佳时间：日落前30分钟。从楼下便利店带瓶啤酒上去。

峨眉山后山徒步路线。不坐索道，从万年寺出发——4小时的竹林徒步，经过瀑布和10多座99%的游客没见过的古寺。早上7点出发。带水和干粮——这条路沿途小店很少。

巷子里的夜宵钵钵鸡。乐山最好吃的钵钵鸡不在饭店——是黄昏时分在老城区街角支一个摊的大妈，两小时就卖光。找本地人在排队的那辆车，不是游客排的。¥2/串，只收现金。
